name: Decidability & the Halting Problem
description: A problem is decidable if a Turing machine halts with the correct answer on every input: after formalizing the problem as a language, one either constructs a total decider or, failing that, reduces the halting problem to it; a valid reduction proves undecidability, as Turing showed the halting problem itself is undecidable.
